title: The current challenges of the Zambian Mining Sector: What is the way forward? – Zambia: News Diggers!
impact_evidence: Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "Inconsistent mineral fiscal policy – Zambia has been very inconsistent with its mining fiscal policy. In the last five years, the country has undertaken more than five reforms to its mining taxation regime." | "Lack of a mineral asset valuation code– Despite a 100 years of mining the country cannot boast of a mineral asset valuation code." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "Lack of strong institutions to provide mining oversight– Zambia lacks strong institutions to provide oversight in terms of mining." | "To curb these schemes, government needs to invest in capacitating state institutions to enhance vigilance of mining operations." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "It is important to note that mining companies engage in tax planning schemes such as misreporting of grade and production values, transfer mispricing etc." | "Some tax provisions of the current taxation regime such as the current VAT system are susceptible to tax planning schemes such as transfer mispricing. This arguably leads to massive revenue leakages in the mining sector." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "Underdeveloped Artisanal and Small-scale Mining (ASM) sector–The ASM sector is currently being faced with several challenges including lack of access to finance, lack of geological information, lack of basic technology in the mining process, and high tax rates. These challenges have inhibited growth of this important sector which continues to be perceived as an alternative livelihood for those that cannot get jobs in the formal sector."
impact_level2: Regulation, Compliance & Institutions || Regulation, Compliance & Institutions || Transparency & Accountability || Livelihoods & Economy
impact_level3: Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness || Enforcement of environmental and social regulations || Corporate transparency and reporting || Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation

title: Gemfields loses its lustre as it swings to an annual loss
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "JSE- and AltX-listed Gemfields swung to a loss of $93.2 million (R1.39 billion) for the year to the end of December compared with a $39.1m profit a year earlier, while revenue plummeted to $34.5m from $216.2m in 2019." | "Gilbertson said that, when combined with the marked stagnation in coloured gemstone trading activity for much of last year, auction revenues fell by almost 89 percent, from 2019's record high of $200.5m to only $22.3m in 2020." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) -> "the restrictions on movement to curb the spread of the pandemic prevented customers from selling their inventory at the world's prestigious gem and jewellery shows, thereby reducing their desire to purchase any further inventory from the group." | ""This liquidity crunch, along with the global travel restrictions, has meant that the group has been unable to hold any traditional auctions since the commercial-quality emerald auction in February 2020," said Lovett." | "Due to Covid-19, the principal operations in Zambia's Kagem mine were suspended for a year between March last year and this month"
impact_level3: Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation || Business opportunities (local supply chains, services)

title: Zambia : FQM donates 31 laptops to Trident students at Nortec
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Human capital development (skills, training, workforce development) -> "This year the mining giant has a total of 38 students pursuing various livelihood support skills development programmes in addition to in-house and customised training initiatives between FQM and the institution." | "A total of 57 students had graduated in different skills training in various fields including heavy equipment repairs, mechanical fitting, metal fabrication and welding; and electrical engineering courses." || Social | Living Conditions & Social Fabric | Education and training opportunities -> "FIRST Quantum Minerals (FQM) has given thirty-one laptops valued at more than K300,000 to Trident Foundation Ltd (TFL) sponsored students at Northern Technical College (Nortec) in Ndola." | "He said the organisation aimed at building local capacity in the areas of agriculture, education, local businesses, and health." | "TFL has been offering education support to the communities in the Kalumbila Mine area of operation from its inception, which includes a scholarship programme."

impact_level3: Human capital development (skills, training, workforce development) || Education and training opportunities
